sourceMovie.title: Priest
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Priest (2011) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Blade II
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Priest clicked for you, Blade II (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and fantasy, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Priest?
faq.answer: Try Blade II, Underworld: Awakening, Day Shift—each shares DNA with Priest while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Priest?
faq.answer: Priest (2011) is best described as Action, Fantasy, Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Priest worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
